input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI Shell app has these problems:
1. GoToAsync("productdetails") throws an exception
2. GoToAsync("//products/active") navigates but nothing visible changes
3. My app takes 3 seconds to start and I have 12 pages in Shell
What's likely wrong in each case and how do I fix them?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI Shell app has these problems:
1. GoToAsync("productdetails") throws an exception
2. GoToAsync("//products/active") navigates but nothing visible changes
3. My app takes 3 seconds to start and I have 12 pages in Shell
What's likely wrong in each case and how do I fix them?

data.content:
Here are the likely causes and fixes:

**1. `GoToAsync("productdetails")` throws an exception**

The route isn't registered. Non-visual (detail) routes must be explicitly registered:

```csharp
// In AppShell constructor or MauiProgram.cs
Routing.RegisterRoute("productdetails", typeof(ProductDetailsPage));
```

Routes defined in XAML (`<ShellContent Route="...">`) are for the visual hierarchy only. Any route you navigate to *programmatically* that isn't part of the visible Shell tree needs `Routing.RegisterRoute`.

---

**2. `GoToAsync("//products/active")` navigates but nothing visibly changes**

The absolute route (`//`) resolves correctly but the Shell tab/section isn't switching visually. This usually means you have **duplicate route names** across Shell items, or the `ShellContent` isn't inside distinct `Tab`/`FlyoutItem` containers. Shell can't disambiguate the target.

Fix: ensure each segment in the hierarchy is unique and properly nested:

```xml
<!-- Bad: ambiguous "active" could exist under multiple parents -->
<FlyoutItem Route="products">
    <ShellContent Route="active" ... />
</FlyoutItem>
<FlyoutItem Route="orders">
    <ShellContent Route="active" ... />  <!-- duplicate! -->
</FlyoutItem>

<!-- Good: unique routes -->
<FlyoutItem Route="products">
    <ShellContent Route="activeProducts" ... />
</FlyoutItem>
<FlyoutItem Route="orders">
    <ShellContent Route="activeOrders" ... />
</FlyoutItem>
```

Also verify the full path matches the actual hierarchy (`FlyoutItem → Tab → ShellContent`). A missing intermediate `Tab` segment will silently fail.

---

**3. 3-second startup with 12 pages in Shell**

Shell creates every page's `ContentPage` at startup by default. Fix with **`ShellContent.ContentTemplate`** to defer creation until the tab is actually visited:

```xml
<!-- Bad: page created immediately -->
<ShellContent Route="settings" ContentPage="{x:Type views:SettingsPage}" />

<!-- Good: page created on first navigation -->
<ShellContent Route="settings"
              ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:SettingsPage}" />
```

Apply `ContentTemplate` (with `DataTemplate`) to all 12 `ShellContent` entries. Only the initially visible page will be instantiated at launch — the rest are created lazily on demand. This alone typically cuts startup time dramatically.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about three common .NET MAUI Shell navigation issues. These are well-known patterns I can answer directly from knowledge without needing to search a codebase.
